assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades require extra compute capacity (maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones)
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains in Option 1 that surge mode creates surge nodes and in Option 2 mentions default percentage-based surge, implying new nodes are created before old ones are deleted

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable >0 instead of relying on maxSurge (drains first, then creates — uses less quota)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends Option 1 with maxSurge=0 and maxUnavailable=1, calling it 'drain-first upgrade' where nodes are deleted before new ones are created

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable >0 means reduced capacity during upgrade — some pods won't have nodes
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Temporary capacity loss during upgrade (each node drains before replacement)' and warns the capacity dip may affect performance

assertions.expectation: Suggests temporarily scaling down non-critical deployments to free up quota for surge nodes
assertions.reason: Option 3 provides detailed kubectl commands to scale down dev/test deployments and non-critical services, specifically to free quota for surge nodes

assertions.expectation: Mentions that reducing maxSurge to 1 minimizes extra quota needed (slower but fits within constraints)
assertions.reason: Option 2 shows maxSurge=1 and explains 'This creates only 1 surge node at a time instead of the default percentage-based surge'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to update surge settings (maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 for zero-extra-capacity mode)
assertions.reason: Multiple gcloud container node-pools update commands are provided with --max-surge-upgrade 0 and --max-unavailable-upgrade 1 parameters

assertions.expectation: Recommends doing the upgrade in off-peak hours when fewer pods are running
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Schedule this during off-peak hours' and 'Schedule during off-peak hours (nights/weekends) when natural capacity is higher'

assertions.expectation: Mentions requesting a temporary quota increase as a faster alternative to a permanent increase
assertions.reason: The response does not mention requesting a temporary quota increase as an alternative - it only provides solutions that work within current quota constraints
